task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a repository named 'pypi-datascience' of type PyPI (Local) in the Artifactory repositories list or creation screen.
Look for a repository named 'pypi-mlops' of type PyPI (Local) in the Artifactory repositories list or creation screen.
Look for a repository named 'pypi-org-proxy' of type PyPI (Remote) in the Artifactory repositories list or creation screen.
Look for a repository named 'pypi-all' of type PyPI (Virtual) in the Artifactory repositories list or creation screen.
Look for groups named 'data-scientists' and 'mlops-engineers' in the Artifactory User Management / Groups section.
Look for permission targets named 'ds-pypi-perms' and 'mlops-pypi-perms' in the Artifactory Permissions section.

integrity.description: The agent used the Artifactory web interface to configure the infrastructure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Artifactory web UI (http://localhost:8082) being used, rather than terminal curl commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected names into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The names of the repositories, groups, and permissions must appear within the actual Artifactory interface, not in a text editor or note-taking app.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the resources in Artifactory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for confirmation messages, populated lists of repositories/groups/permissions, or active creation forms in the Artifactory UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Artifactory instance is at http://localhost:8082. The agent must create four PyPI repositories: 'pypi-datascience' (Local), 'pypi-mlops' (Local), 'pypi-org-proxy' (Remote), and 'pypi-all' (Virtual). The agent must also create two groups: 'data-scientists' and 'mlops-engineers', and two permission targets: 'ds-pypi-perms' and 'mlops-pypi-perms'.
